description: Parse models/endpoints.json into structured partition/service/endpoint data
notes: 5 tests, OnceLock caching, lazy-loaded from models/endpoints.json

description: Resolve endpoints using partition data: aws, aws-cn, aws-us-gov, aws-iso, aws-iso-b
notes: 6 tests, two-pass lookup: explicit regions then regionRegex matching

description: Service-specific endpoint overrides from endpoints.json (e.g., S3 has custom hostname patterns)
notes: 7 tests, per-region hostname overrides, service defaults

description: Global services (IAM, STS, Route53, CloudFront) resolve to correct global endpoints
STS regional endpoints: support both global (sts.amazonaws.com) and regional (sts.us-east-1.amazonaws.com)
notes: 5 tests, STS is regionalized by default despite having partitionEndpoint
7 tests, DNS-compatible bucket names get virtual-hosted style, bucket prefix stripped from URI
6 tests, buckets with dots or non-DNS-compatible names use path-style

description: Dual-stack endpoint support: --use-dualstack-endpoint flag resolves to *.dualstack.* URLs
notes: Flag added to GlobalArgs, resolver uses variants from endpoints.json

description: FIPS endpoint support: --use-fips-endpoint flag resolves to fips-prefixed URLs
notes: Flag added to GlobalArgs, resolver uses variants from endpoints.json, combinable with dualstack

description: --endpoint-url flag overrides resolved endpoint (already implemented, add tests)
notes: 4 tests, override bypasses endpoint resolution entirely

description: China partition endpoints: *.amazonaws.com.cn for cn-north-1, cn-northwest-1
GovCloud partition endpoints: *.amazonaws.com for us-gov-east-1, us-gov-west-1
notes: 7 tests, partition-specific hostnames for us-gov-* regions
